notes: Latest official TWSE cash-session close available prior to the 2026-04-13 pre-open capture.
TAIEX closed higher on 2026-04-10, with technology-related indices leading; the TWSE Taiwan Technology Index rose 1.98% and the Semiconductor Index rose 2.07%.
Official three-institution trading value snapshot for 2026-04-10 showed foreign investors remained net buyers by NT$28.81bn.
The positive total reading supports the view that Taiwan risk appetite stayed constructive into the weekend, even though投信轉為賣超。

headline_risk_context.headline: Taiwan enters Monday pre-open with no single new shock: AI demand remains the local support, while the fragile Middle East truce and inflation/yield pressure remain the main macro overlays.
notes: CNBC described U.S. equities as finishing a strong week while traders monitored the fragile Iran ceasefire and oil volatility; tech outperformed broadly.
Reuters snippets indicated March U.S. CPI rose 3.3% year on year and highlighted that inflation/rate concerns remain a valuation cap even as sentiment improved.
Focus Taiwan reported Taiwan March exports hit a record US$80.18 billion with AI-related demand still strong, while Reuters reported TSMC Q1 revenue rose 35% year on year ahead of April 16 earnings.

secondary_drivers: Taiwan AI-demand proof points strengthened after March exports hit a record and TSMC reported Q1 revenue above market expectations
Middle East truce durability and oil-price direction still shape the macro risk premium for Asia
US March CPI / Fed path / Treasury-yield sensitivity remains the main cap on valuation expansion
likely_affected_groups: Large-cap semiconductor and AI supply-chain names
Export-oriented electronics and broad Taiwan tech ETFs
Old-economy cyclicals sensitive to oil/input costs
Rate- and FX-sensitive sectors if yields or USD move sharply
market_driver_summary.why_it_matters: Into the 2026-04-13 Taiwan pre-open, there is no single fresh shock dominating everything. Instead, the local tape is being supported by concrete AI-demand evidence (record Taiwan exports, strong TSMC revenue) while the macro overlay remains hostage to whether the Middle East truce really holds and whether higher inflation keeps US yields elevated. That combination argues for a still constructive but not clean risk-on setup.
market_driver_summary.what_would_invalidate: A visible breakdown in the truce that re-accelerates oil, a renewed hawkish repricing in US rates after inflation/Fed commentary, or disappointing semiconductor guidance (especially from TSMC this week) would invalidate the mild risk-on read.

persistent_watch: US inflation / Fed path / Treasury yields
Middle East geopolitics, shipping risk, and oil-price trajectory
AI / semiconductor demand confirmation from Taiwan exports, TSMC, and Nvidia-linked supply chain
USD/TWD and broader Asia FX moves tied to capital-flow repricing
Any renewed tariff / industrial-policy headlines that directly affect Taiwan supply chains

current_high_priority.title: AI demand proof-points: Taiwan exports + TSMC revenue into Apr. 16 earnings
current_high_priority.why: This is the clearest Taiwan-specific fundamental support right now. Record March exports and strong TSMC revenue help explain why foreign money kept returning to Taiwan tech even with macro noise still present.
current_high_priority.expected_actionability: High — directly changes how much room the market gives AI / semiconductor leaders this week

current_high_priority.title: Middle East truce durability and oil around the high-90s
current_high_priority.why: This remains the fastest path to a risk-on continuation or a violent reversal. Taiwan tends to benefit when the oil/geopolitical risk premium compresses, but that support is fragile.
current_high_priority.expected_actionability: High — index-level risk appetite and foreign flow sensitivity

current_high_priority.title: US CPI / Fed / yield ceiling on multiples
current_high_priority.why: Even if Taiwan fundamentals are good, a hotter inflation read and hawkish-rate repricing can cap semiconductor multiple expansion and pressure FX-sensitive flows.
current_high_priority.expected_actionability: High — especially for large-cap tech and Taiwan-dollar-sensitive positioning

deprioritized.title: Early-April tariff shock as the standalone dominant narrative
deprioritized.why: Still a structural risk, but the last several trading days were more clearly driven by oil/geopolitics, inflation-rate repricing, and AI demand confirmation than by any fresh tariff escalation.

deprioritized.title: Pure defensive commodity chase
deprioritized.why: That was more relevant during the immediate war-scare phase; Taiwan price action has since rotated back toward technology leadership.

surprise_buckets: Truce breaks / Strait risk re-prices / oil spikes again → broad Asia risk-off
US yields jump harder than expected after inflation or Fed talk → tech valuation compression
TSMC guidance disappoints despite strong monthly sales → AI chain de-rating
Sharp USD/TWD or wider Asia FX stress → foreign-flow reversal even without bad local fundamentals
